title: Long-Running Operations in fabio
summary: Many Fabric operations are async (return 202). fabio polls them automatically. Use --wait for job operations.

fabio_behavior.automatic_lro_polling: Create, getDefinition, and updateDefinition operations return 202 + Location header. fabio polls every 2s (respects Retry-After, capped at 60s) until Succeeded/Failed, max 120s.
fabio_behavior.job_operations_with_wait: Notebook run, pipeline run, and job-scheduler run-on-demand return immediately by default. Add --wait to poll until completion.
fabio_behavior.lro_timeout: Default max LRO wait is 120s. Override with --lro-timeout <seconds>.

patterns_for_agents.wait_for_completion: fabio data-pipeline run --workspace $WS --id $ID --wait --timeout 600 → polls until done
patterns_for_agents.check_status_later: fabio operation get-state --operation-id <ID> → check LRO status manually

what_agents_should_do: For create/update operations: fabio handles LRO automatically — just call the command
For job operations: use --wait if you need the result, omit --wait for async fire-and-forget
If you get TIMEOUT: the operation may still be running. Use 'fabio operation get-state' to check
After timeout without --cancel-on-timeout: operation continues server-side

relevant_flags.--wait: Poll job until terminal state (for run/execute operations)
relevant_flags.--timeout <seconds>: Max wait time for --wait (default 600s for jobs)
relevant_flags.--lro-timeout <seconds>: Max wait for create/definition LRO operations (default 120s)
relevant_flags.--cancel-on-timeout: Cancel the job if --timeout is reached
